new_value: {"description":"Deploy OpenClaw AI assistant and Ollama inference server as separate LXC containers on Proxmox (nuc13). OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202): 2 cores, 4GB RAM, 20GB disk — NEEDS INSTALLER VERIFICATION AND MANUAL ONBOARDING. Open WebUI on dockerhost — DEPLOYED AND WORKING at chat.home.alborworld.com. old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.11","title":"Add dedicated VLAN for OpenClaw LXC","description":"OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202) sits on the default vmbr0 bridge with no VLAN isolation. Per merox.dev deployment guide, it should have a dedicated VLAN in Proxmox for network segmentation. Update tofu/proxmox/openclaw/main.tf with VLAN tag and adjust firewall rules accordingly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T05:39:35Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T05:39:35Z"}

new_value: {"description":"OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202) sits on the default vmbr0 bridge with no VLAN isolation. Per merox.dev deployment guide, it should have a dedicated VLAN in Proxmox for network segmentation. However, the existing iptables rules (default DROP) + Tailscale mesh already provide strong isolation. The practical security gain of adding a VLAN is minimal for a homelab — revisit if untrusted devices join the LAN or if the threat model changes. Would require: new VLAN on router, Proxmox bridge VLAN tag in Tofu, static IP on new subnet, firewall rule updates.","notes":"2026-02-23: Assessed as low priority. Existing iptables (default DROP) + Tailscale provide equivalent isolation. Static DHCP reservation set on router for LXC IP. Deprioritized — revisit if network trust model changes.","priority":4,"status":"open"}

new_value: {"status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"homelab-rbo.14","title":"Add audio transcription with Whisper to OpenClaw","description":"Add voice message transcription using faster-whisper so OpenClaw can process audio/ogg messages.

Steps:
1. Install faster-whisper runtime in a Python venv on the OpenClaw LXC (VMID 202), plus ffmpeg
2. Create ~/bin/transcribe_ogg.py script using WhisperModel('base', device='cpu', compute_type='int8')
3. Hook into OpenClaw inbound message flow: detect audio/ogg media, run transcription, replace message text
4. Quality tuning: vad_filter=True, consider model size (base→small→medium), set language for speed
5. Optional: enable TTS responses for full voice loop

Key details:
- Use faster-whisper (CPU, int8) as the runtime — easiest for LXC without GPU
- Script takes an audio file path, outputs transcribed text to stdout
- Integration point is the inbound message handler/middleware — add conditional for audio/* mimeType
- Model 'base' for speed, upgrade to 'small'/'medium' for accuracy later","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T07:17:33Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T07:17:33Z"}

new_value: Added Ansible IaC for Whisper transcription: faster-whisper venv, transcribe_ogg.py template, wrapper script, systemd PATH update, architecture docs updated
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.15","title":"Fix systemctl --user bus error in OpenClaw LXC","description":"OpenClaw gateway fails to start with: 'Gateway service check failed: Error: systemctl --user unavailable: Failed to connect to bus: No medium found'. This is because the openclaw service runs as a system service (not user), but OpenClaw internally tries to use systemctl --user which requires a D-Bus user session. Need to either enable lingering for the openclaw user (loginctl enable-linger) or configure XDG_RUNTIME_DIR so the user bus is available inside the LXC.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T07:36:39Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T07:36:39Z"}

new_value: Fixed by adding XDG_RUNTIME_DIR and DBUS_SESSION_BUS_ADDRESS to systemd service template (resolved via Ansible getent), plus loginctl enable-linger
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.16","title":"Change TTS provider to ElevenLabs","description":"Switch OpenClaw's TTS provider from current (likely OpenAI) to ElevenLabs Free. Steps: 1) Get ElevenLabs API key, add to SOPS secrets. 2) Choose a voice in ElevenLabs → Voices, copy Voice ID. 3) Update OpenClaw TTS config (openclaw.json or Ansible-managed config) with ElevenLabs provider, API key, and voice ID. 4) Optionally create voice presets (e.g. calm, energetic). 5) Restart OpenClaw and test.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T07:57:25Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T07:57:25Z"}

{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.17","title":"Investigate Proxmox firewall rules for OpenClaw LXC via Tofu","description":"Check if the bpg/proxmox Tofu provider supports defining firewall rules at the container level. If so, codify rules equivalent to the current iptables setup (default DROP inbound except from Proxmox host, restrict outbound to 80/443) in the OpenClaw Tofu stack. Proxmox-level firewall is stronger than in-container iptables since it can't be bypassed if the container is compromised.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T20:34:39Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:34:39Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"bpg/proxmox provider (v0.70+) fully supports container-level firewall via two resources: proxmox_virtual_environment_firewall_options (default policy DROP/ACCEPT per container) and proxmox_virtual_environment_firewall_rules (specific inbound/outbound rules with ports, protocols, source IPs). Rules enforced at hypervisor level — can't be bypassed from inside LXC. No firewall config exists in the stack yet. Implementation: add both resources to tofu/proxmox/openclaw/main.tf targeting container_id=202 on nuc13. Need to account for Tailscale UDP, DNS, and Ollama connectivity."}
old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.17","title":"Investigate Proxmox firewall rules for OpenClaw LXC via Tofu","description":"Check if the bpg/proxmox Tofu provider supports defining firewall rules at the container level. If so, codify rules equivalent to the current iptables setup (default DROP inbound except from Proxmox host, restrict outbound to 80/443) in the OpenClaw Tofu stack. Proxmox-level firewall is stronger than in-container iptables since it can't be bypassed if the container is compromised.","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T20:34:39Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:34:51Z"}

new_value: Proxmox hypervisor-level firewall applied via Tofu. Default DROP inbound/outbound with allowlists for SSH (LAN), Tailscale WireGuard/STUN, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS. Verified Tailscale connectivity post-apply.
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"homelab-rbo.18","title":"Verify and enable Proxmox cluster-level firewall","description":"The Proxmox cluster firewall (cluster.fw) has no enable option set, meaning per-container firewall rules in 202.fw may not be enforced. Need to: 1) Test if container-level rules work without cluster firewall enabled 2) If not, enable cluster-level firewall via pvesh/API 3) Consider managing this in Tofu. Risk: enabling cluster firewall could affect all VMs/LXCs on nuc13 if they don't have their own firewall configs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"alessandro@alborworld.com","created_at":"2026-02-23T20:44:49Z","created_by":"Alessandro Bortolussi","updated_at":"2026-02-23T20:44:49Z"}

new_value: Verified: Proxmox per-container firewall (202.fw enable:1) IS enforced without cluster-level firewall. Test: flushed in-container iptables, port 853 still blocked by Proxmox layer, port 80 still allowed. Cluster-level firewall is optional — per-container enable flag is sufficient. No action needed.
